mechanism_narrative: A sustained federal funds rate below 0.25% compresses equity discount rates and lowers corporate borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher when FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 10.0% signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances where UNRATE exceeds 10.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP and monthly auto sales confirms broad consumer demand recovery, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS yields negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-crisis rebound reflects factory utilization normalization and supply chain stabilization, generating positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with month-over-month INDPRO changes over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts signal durable consumer confidence and construction sector expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 65% of months where HOUST increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 255 trigger inflation risk premium repricing, causing negative equity returns over the next quarter as real yields rise.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 255.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rate indicates labor market tightening and wage pressure, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline yields negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Auto sales volume exceeding 450 million dollars during fiscal stimulus phases reflects liquidity-driven consumer spending, generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of weeks where RSAFS exceeds 450 million dollars during stimulus periods.
